principles: The CMS is a constrained editorial system, not a page builder.
Only existing text-bearing and metadata-bearing surfaces may be mutated.
Public browsers consume published content through a read-only contract.
Write access, authentication, sessions, secrets, and audit logs stay on a separate admin origin.
Design tokens, layouts, route wiring, analytics, security, and executable code remain developer-owned.

ownershipClasses.title: Client-editable text
ownershipClasses.description: End-client editors may change the approved text fields through constrained controls.

ownershipClasses.title: Operator-controlled
ownershipClasses.description: Only trained publishers or internal operators may change this text or metadata because it affects trust, legal accuracy, routing, or publication state.

ownershipClasses.title: Developer-owned
ownershipClasses.description: Structure, rendering logic, runtime behavior, and technical configuration remain outside the client CMS.

mutationPrimitives.label: Short plain text
mutationPrimitives.description: Single-line copy such as headings, labels, or concise helper text.

mutationPrimitives.label: Long plain text
mutationPrimitives.description: Paragraph-length plain text with no markup.

mutationPrimitives.label: Constrained rich text
mutationPrimitives.description: Very small allowlist for legal or editorial body copy.

mutationPrimitives.label: Repeatable fixed-template list
mutationPrimitives.description: Editors may add, remove, and reorder items only within a fixed template the public site already knows how to render.

mutationPrimitives.label: SEO snippet
mutationPrimitives.description: Metadata text such as SEO title, description, or social-preview fallback copy.

mutationPrimitives.label: Alt text or caption
mutationPrimitives.description: Accessibility text for already-approved media assets.

mutationPrimitives.label: Route picker
mutationPrimitives.description: Internal destination selected from an allowlisted set of existing public routes.

mutationPrimitives.label: External link allowlist
mutationPrimitives.description: External destination selected from an operator-managed allowlist, not typed as a free URL.

mutationPrimitives.label: Publication control
mutationPrimitives.description: Draft, review, publish, archive, and revert metadata for editorial items.

mutationPrimitives.label: Developer locked
mutationPrimitives.description: Surface is intentionally outside CMS mutation scope.

linkPolicies.label: No link editing
linkPolicies.description: Surface does not expose editable links.

linkPolicies.label: Internal route picker only
linkPolicies.description: Destination must be chosen from a fixed list of public routes.

linkPolicies.label: External allowlist
linkPolicies.description: Destination must come from an operator-maintained allowlist such as Instagram or the ICO.

linkPolicies.label: Operator managed
linkPolicies.description: Link target is managed by publishers or maintainers, not end-client editors.

forbiddenCapabilities: arbitrary media upload for public use without governance

roles.title: Invited client editor
roles.description: Can draft and revise approved editable copy fields, but cannot publish or change trust-critical configuration.

roles.title: Approver and publisher
roles.description: Owns review, trust-critical text, publication state, and content rollback.

roles.title: Technical maintainer
roles.description: Owns schema, mappings, runtime, deployment, and emergency recovery.

seedSources.notes: Seeds footer support copy, utility copy, and route-bound CTA text without exposing navigation structure editing.
Seeds organisation name, tagline, service-area language, and public identity text.
Primary source for static-route intros, sections, CTAs, helper copy, and trust callouts.
Preserves canonical homepage section ids and CTA references.
Preserves slugs, audience metadata, and programme-level editorial copy.
Seeds programme detail templates and route-specific supporting copy.
Preserves session slugs, recurrence linkage, and operational metadata.
Seeds fixed-template session detail text around the canonical session data.
Preserves publication status, slugs, taxonomy, and item-level detail copy.
Preserves FAQ group ownership for route families.
Seeds helper text, field labels, reason sets, and submission copy while keeping validation/runtime logic outside CMS scope.
Legal and policy content remains operator-controlled even when stored in the CMS.
Site policy and legal boundaries stay operator-controlled.
SEO fields are imported for review and publication control, not open end-client editing by default.
Only alt text, captions, and disclosure text are in scope for future CMS editing.
